subject: ENGLISH
title: Read the Picture Graph
objective: Interpret a pictograph using its title, categories, symbols, and key.
introduction: A pictograph uses symbols to represent data. Always read the key before counting values. You will explore examples, sort cards, practice, and answer five questions.

vocabulary.definition: A key word used in the lesson “Read the Picture Graph”.
A key word used in the lesson “Read the Picture Graph”.
A key word used in the lesson “Read the Picture Graph”.

activities.instruction: Study the idea and listen to the narration.
activities.content: A pictograph uses symbols to represent data. Always read the key before counting values.
activities.accessibilityAlternative: Text alternative: A pictograph uses symbols to represent data. Always read the key before counting values.

activities.instruction: Explore each learning token, then read the examples.

examples: Key: one fruit icon = two votes
Compare categories using the same key

activities.accessibilityAlternative: A numbered list of the same examples is available for keyboard and screen-reader use.

activities.instruction: Sort each card into “fits the lesson” or “does not fit.”

fits: Key: one fruit icon = two votes
Compare categories using the same key
doesNotFit: Assuming one icon always equals one
Comparing icon size instead of value

feedback.correct: Your groups follow the lesson rule.
feedback.retry: Use the explanation, not color or position.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Tap an item and then tap a labeled category; dragging is optional.

activities.instruction: If one icon means 2 votes, what do 3 icons mean?

feedback.retry: Read the lesson rule again. 3 votes and 5 votes do not fit.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: All choices are visible text and selectable with TalkBack.

activities.instruction: Match each example to the lesson idea.

pairs.left: Key: one fruit icon = two votes
Compare categories using the same key

feedback.retry: Return to the explanation and compare one pair at a time.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: A list-based matching control replaces drag gestures.

activities.instruction: Complete the guided review in order.

feedback.correct: You are ready for the five-question check.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Up and down buttons provide an alternative to dragging.

items.prompt: Which statement best matches “Read the Picture Graph”?

options.text: A pictograph uses symbols to represent data. Always read the key before counting values.
Assuming one icon always equals one

items.explanation: The correct response is “A pictograph uses symbols to represent data. Always read the key before counting values.” because it follows the lesson explanation: A pictograph uses symbols to represent data. Always read the key before counting values.

options.text: Key: one fruit icon = two votes
Assuming one icon always equals one

items.explanation: The correct response is “Key: one fruit icon = two votes” because it follows the lesson explanation: A pictograph uses symbols to represent data. Always read the key before counting values.

items.prompt: Which choice does not follow the lesson idea?

options.text: Assuming one icon always equals one
Key: one fruit icon = two votes

items.explanation: The correct response is “Assuming one icon always equals one” because it follows the lesson explanation: A pictograph uses symbols to represent data. Always read the key before counting values.

items.prompt: If one icon means 2 votes, what do 3 icons mean?
items.explanation: The correct response is “6 votes” because it follows the lesson explanation: A pictograph uses symbols to represent data. Always read the key before counting values.

items.prompt: Which example should be used in the final review?

options.text: Compare categories using the same key
Comparing icon size instead of value
Assuming one icon always equals one

items.explanation: The correct response is “Compare categories using the same key” because it follows the lesson explanation: A pictograph uses symbols to represent data. Always read the key before counting values.

assetSpecs.prompt: Friendly Grade 3 cartoon storybook concept board for Read the Picture Graph; rounded shapes, warm dark-brown outlines, tropical palette, no embedded text, uncluttered small-screen layout. Compose overlays provide labels and interactive targets.
assetSpecs.contentDescription: Five connected learning tokens for exploring examples in Read the Picture Graph.
